commit.message:
Make source/bucket easy to switch between environments

- Add source as optional first argument to all log commands
- Support convenient aliases: dev, prod, staging, etc.
- Override configured default source when specified
- Add helpful examples in command descriptions

Now you can easily switch between environments:
  bslog tail dev                # tail development logs
  bslog tail prod -n 50         # tail production logs
  bslog errors dev --since 1h   # dev errors from last hour
  bslog search "timeout" prod   # search in production

The source argument always overrides the configured default,
making it easy to quickly check different environments.

Add CLAUDE.md for Claude Code guidance

Provides comprehensive documentation for Claude Code instances working with this codebase, including:
- Development commands for building, testing, and code quality
- Architecture overview with directory structure and authentication flow
- Query processing pipeline from GraphQL syntax to ClickHouse SQL
- Testing strategy and code style guidelines

Add verbose flag and improve log output

- Add -v/--verbose flag to all log commands
- Hide SQL query output by default (only show with -v flag)
- Show full structured log data by parsing raw JSON field
- Extract and display all fields from log entries
- Improve formatter to show complete log information

Now logs show all available data fields by default, and SQL queries
are only displayed when using the verbose flag.

Improve error messages for missing Query API credentials

- Add detailed error message showing which environment variables are set/missing
- Show current environment status with checkmarks and X marks
- Provide clear instructions on how to fix the issue
- Include steps to get Query API credentials if needed

The error message now clearly diagnoses authentication issues and
provides actionable steps to resolve them.

Remove dotenv debug messages and fix authentication

- Replace dotenv package with manual .env parsing to avoid debug output
- Add Query API credentials to shell configuration
- Fix tests to include new team_id and table_name fields
- Ensure all authentication methods work properly

The CLI now works cleanly without debug messages and properly
handles both Telemetry and Query API authentication.

Document authentication architecture in README

- Explain why both authentication systems are required
- Detail what each authentication method is used for
- Add verification steps to test both APIs
- Provide complete setup example with all environment variables
- Clarify the security separation between source metadata and log data access

Fix Query API authentication and improve error handling

- Add support for Query API credentials via environment variables
- Fix table name generation to use team_id instead of source_id
- Add clear error messages guiding users to create Query API credentials
- Update README to clearly distinguish between Telemetry and Query APIs
- Add team_id and table_name fields to Source interface
- Implement proper Basic authentication for Query API

The CLI now correctly uses:
- Bearer token for Telemetry API (listing sources)
- Basic auth with username/password for Query API (querying logs)

Make README more professional

- Remove all emojis from section headers and content
- Replace arrow symbols with standard greater-than signs
- Remove roadmap section to keep documentation focused
- Maintain professional tone throughout documentation

Switch to Bun runtime and pnpm package manager

- Replace npm with pnpm for faster, more efficient package management
- Configure project to use Bun runtime (>=1.0.0)
- Update all dependencies to latest versions
- Add comprehensive .gitignore for build outputs
- Update README with Bun/pnpm installation instructions
- Remove postinstall script and package-lock.json
- Add unit tests for core utilities

Initial commit: Set up bslog CLI with Biome linting

- Initialize npm project for Better Stack log query CLI
- Add Biome for code formatting and linting with strict rules
- Apply initial formatting fixes to all source files
- Configure common linting rules for code quality
Initial commit

commit.message:
Complete environment switching implementation for all commands

Updated remaining commands to support global environment options:
- jobs.ts: Added environment switching support
- status.ts: Added environment switching support
- config.ts: Fixed parseInt radix for proper linting

All commands now consistently support:
- --env prod|dev for environment switching
- --dev-port for custom dev server ports
- Proper global option inheritance from parent command

This completes the environment switching feature across the entire CLI.

Document verbose flag and performance features in README

Added documentation for:
- --verbose/-v flag for debug output control
- Performance monitoring and debugging section
- Debug information details (API timing, chunk processing, etc.)
- Performance optimization features (parallel requests, early termination)
- Updated global options to include --version flag
- Usage examples with verbose flag
Bump version to 0.9.1

Add verbose flag and fix version display

Features:
- Add -v/--verbose flag to list command for debug output control
- Debug messages now only show with --verbose flag (clean output by default)
- Fix CLI version display to show correct 0.9.0 version
- Add debug() method to InngestClient for controlled logging
- Update constructor to accept verbose option

Usage:
- inngest list --status Failed --details (clean output)
- inngest list --status Failed --details --verbose (debug output)
- inngest --version (now shows 0.9.0)

Update default dev server port from 3000 to 8288

- Change default dev server port to match actual dev environment URL
- Update CLI help text and README documentation
- Maintain backward compatibility with --dev-port override option

Add performance optimizations and environment switching support

Performance improvements:
- Implement parallel time-based chunk fetching (6 concurrent requests)
- Reduce chunk size from 24h to 12h for better parallelism
- Add early termination when enough events are found
- Smart result limiting (5x desired limit to account for filtering)

Environment switching:
- Add --env flag to switch between prod (default) and dev
- Add --dev-port flag for custom dev server ports
- Support INNGEST_DEV_SERVER_URL and INNGEST_DEV_SERVER_PORT env vars
- Update all commands to use global environment options
- Add environment indicator in CLI output

Code quality:
- Fix TypeScript compilation errors with proper type assertions
- Update tests to match new API limits (100 vs 500)
- Replace 'any' types with 'unknown' for better type safety
- Fix linting issues and format code consistently

Documentation:
- Add environment configuration section to README
- Document global options and environment variables
- Include usage examples for both prod and dev environments

docs: Update README title and version to 0.9.0

- Change title to "Inngest CLI" and link Inngest to inngest.com
- Update package version to 0.9.0 for pre-release
- Restore --all option documentation that exists in code
- Improve --details examples with proper --hours usage

Preparing for npm publish as beta release.

feat: Add --details flag with input/output data display and deep search

- Add --details flag to list command for full run inspection
- Implement input data display with event data caching
- Add time-based chunking for deep historical search
- Support pagination to find failed/cancelled runs across multiple time periods
- Display both input and error details for debugging failed runs
- Use proper color coding: cyan for input, red for errors

Addresses the need to easily inspect failed runs with complete context
including the original event data that triggered the run.

feat: Add configurable time range filtering for better incident analysis

Major improvements:
- Add time range filtering with --hours, --after, --before options
- Default to 24-hour lookback when filtering by status (was 7 days)
- Enable searching historical data for failed runs and incident analysis
- Add comprehensive time filtering examples to README
- Update help menu with clear time range option descriptions

New filtering capabilities:
- inngest list --status Failed                    # Last 24 hours (default)
- inngest list --status Failed --hours 72         # Last 3 days
- inngest list --after "2024-01-01T09:00:00Z"     # Since specific time
- inngest list --before "2024-01-01T17:00:00Z"    # Before specific time

This resolves the issue where failed runs weren't showing up because they
were outside the default time window. Users can now:
1. Find recent failed runs easily (24h default)
2. Conduct incident analysis with custom time ranges
3. Search historical data for patterns and debugging
4. Combine time ranges with function name filtering

Updated tests to reflect new API behavior with time ranges.
All 42 tests pass.

docs: Update README and CLI help with function name features

Documentation improvements:
- Add comprehensive section on Function Name Filtering
- Include practical examples of --function-name filtering
- Show example CLI output with readable function names
- Update help text to show available status options upfront
- Add function-specific monitoring examples
- Document partial matching capabilities (e.g., "sweeper", "embeddings/reconcile")

Help menu improvements:
- Clearer status option description: "Available: Running, Completed, Failed, Cancelled"
- Better function filtering descriptions with examples
- More descriptive option help text throughout

Examples added:
- inngest list --function-name "sweeper"
- inngest list --status Failed --function-name "system.jobs"
- Complete table output showing sweetistics-system.jobs.sweeper vs UUIDs

feat: Add function names to runs table and filtering

Major improvements:
- Display actual function names instead of UUIDs in runs table
- Extract function names from events data (e.g., "sweetistics-system.jobs.sweeper")
- Add --function-name option for clearer filtering by function name
- Support partial matching for function names (e.g., "sweeper", "system.jobs")
- Enhance existing --function option to work with both IDs and names
- Fallback to function_id if function_name is not available
- All 42 tests pass, no breaking changes

Examples:
- inngest list --function-name "sweeper"
- inngest list --function-name "embeddings/reconcile"
- inngest list --function "system.jobs" --status Running

docs: Remove emojis and enhance feature descriptions

- Replace emoji-based feature list with detailed descriptions
- Add technical implementation details (TypeScript, Zod validation, testing)
- Emphasize error handling and user experience improvements
- Maintain all usage examples and API reference information

fix: Update API endpoints and remove dotenv noise

Major improvements:
- Fixed API endpoints to use /v1/events and correct data structure
- Updated listRuns to query events first, then fetch runs by ID
- Fixed getRun to extract data from response.data.data
- Suppressed dotenv injection messages in CLI output
- Updated all tests to match new API behavior
- Added comprehensive error handling with user-friendly messages
- All 42 tests pass and CLI functions correctly

The CLI now works with the actual Inngest API structure:
- Lists runs by discovering them through recent events
- Supports status and function filtering
- Shows clean, formatted output without noise
- Provides detailed run information and cancellation features

improve: Enhanced error handling with detailed user guidance

- Add comprehensive error messages for missing INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY
- Reorganize API client error interceptor to prioritize specific HTTP status codes
- Provide helpful troubleshooting guidance for 404, 401, 403 errors with emojis
- Update tests to match new error message patterns
- Fix formatting with Biome to ensure consistent code style

feat: Complete Inngest CLI implementation with modern tooling

- Built comprehensive TypeScript CLI for Inngest job management
- Added Zod 4.x for runtime API validation with strict schemas
- Implemented Vitest testing with 42 passing tests and full coverage
- Configured Biome linting with strict rules (no any types)
- Created complete command set: status, list, watch, cancel, jobs
- Added real-time monitoring with configurable polling intervals
- Implemented robust error handling with detailed feedback
- Created comprehensive technical specification in docs/spec.md
- Added test script for CLI functionality validation
- All dependencies updated to latest versions
- Zero linting errors, all tests passing, production ready

Commands:
- inngest status: Get run/event status with detailed info
- inngest list: List and filter runs with pagination
- inngest watch: Real-time monitoring with live updates
- inngest cancel: Individual and bulk cancellation with safety
- inngest jobs: Detailed job execution analysis

Features:
- Runtime validation with Zod schemas
- Type-safe API client with comprehensive error handling
- Rich terminal output with colors and formatted tables
- Environment-based configuration with validation
- Graceful error handling for network and API issues
- Complete test coverage with mocked API responses
Initial commit

commit.message: Fix code formatting for consistency
fix: normalize bulk cancellation response
Bump version to 0.9.2

Fix help command to behave like standard Unix CLI

- Remove error message when displaying help
- Ensure --help exits with code 0
- Clean up exitOverride handler to properly handle commander.helpDisplayed
- Remove unnecessary output configuration that was causing issues

feat: add JSON output format support for all commands

- Add --format json flag to all commands (list, status, jobs, cancel, cancellation-status)
- Implement JSON preparation functions in display utilities
- Add comprehensive tests for JSON output formatting
- Update README with JSON format examples and usage patterns
- Enable programmatic integration with jq and other tools

Fix linting issues

- Apply biome formatter and linter fixes
- Use optional chaining for error.message check
- Remove unused import from cli.test.ts

Add CLI behavior tests and fix no-args help exit code

- Add comprehensive tests for CLI help and version commands
- Test that --help and --version exit with code 0
- Test environment and dev-port option validation
- Fix no-arguments case to exit with code 0 when displaying help
- Ensure CLI behaves like standard Unix tools
